title: polygala penaea subsp. portoricensis
intro: Polygala penaea subsp. portoricensis, also known as Portorican milkwort, is a plant species belonging to the Polygalaceae family, native to the island of Puerto Rico. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it beneficial for various health issues such as fever reduction, wound healing, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Portorican milkwort include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve immune function, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities. Herbal preparations of Portorican milkwort can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by using the roots to create a decoction, which can be used to treat various ailments.
